notes: Codex variant: a conversation runs one turn, exits cleanly (Ctrl-D → process_exited), then is relaunched via `codex resume <uuid>`. Codex APPENDS to the SAME rollout file (verified on 0.130.0), so the daemon-side session_id is STABLE across both process lifetimes — ONE session id, TWO PIDs. The resumed half must re-bind the SAME session_id (not a new one); v2 phases pin to v1 via same_session_as so a co-occurring different session can't satisfy the match. Mirrors claudecode/session-resume's single-session-two-lifetimes shape, adding an explicit process_exited phase between the lifetimes and the same_session_as identity pin codex needs.

text: First launch: session appears in ready
First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Clean exit (Ctrl-D) after the first turn: process_exited for the session
Spec: 'Same session_id reappears (not a new session row)' — codex resume reopens the SAME rollout, so the SAME session id re-binds to a new PID in ready
Spec: 'ready → working on the first new turn' — the second turn drives the SAME session to working
Second turn completes back to ready on the SAME session — each lifetime's arc is independently correct
